Product Marketing Meeting.mp4.task: GTM teams need to confirm their support for specific corporate events by commenting on the issue.
PMMs should try to help the corporate events team track down information and get commitments from campaign managers.
Platform team to assist with tracking down information and providing support for corporate events.
Review the existing content from 14.0 and identify what can be reused for the GA announcement.
Add new features to complement the reused 14.0 content for the GA announcement.
Bucket similar features together (e.g., 'research-based user experience').
Assign an excitement level (1-3) to each feature.
Determine the top five features based on excitement levels and stack rank them.
Move some items from Docs to Spreadsheet for further analysis and prioritization.
Create a PR (Pull Request) driven process to determine the top features.
Review and aggregate the list of announcements for the last 12 months, due by Tuesday.
Create a top five list based on the aggregated announcement information, due by Tuesday.
Sami will add a top 5 plan to the spreadsheet.
Review the competitive sheet and compare against relevant competitors for each stage.
Complete an assessment of 5-15 features through the lens of market competition (with some being unique to GitLab or competitor only).
Call out platforms that build themselves (e.g., GitHub, Azure DevOps) and have zero capabilities in certain areas.
Describe capabilities in a value-based way, not just by product stage.
The team will review the infographic created by the design team.
Determine and reconcile an alternative set of stages.
Update the page content to make it easier for users to understand the features.
Channel inner Ash Withers' style for creating catchy and nuanced messaging.
Refine the tone and language of the marketing materials to ensure parity in tone and message.
Finalize the revised slogan ('More speed, less risk with confidence'), due at the end of the day.
